text: Phase 1 is a scheduled job, not a service. Hugin tasks + Munin can handle it without a new component.
Deterministic scanners (npm audit, trivy, semgrep) should lead. AI STRIDE should be change-triggered, not weekly.
Phase 1-2-3 progression is unrealistic. Phase 3 (pen testing with staging harness) probably never for a 2-Pi personal infra.
Single weekly cadence is too coarse. Cadence should be per-check-class: event-driven for deploy-adjacent, daily for deps, monthly for STRIDE.
Security risk of the security service itself is underaddressed. Scanner + fixer + task dispatch in one process increases blast radius.
Operational cost for single operator is understated even after acknowledging overhead.
Name 'Syn' is decent but over-precise for a proposal that is too broad. Semantic overlap with Heimdall (watchman).
Munin publication contract missing: canonical key layout, required fields, finding identity across runs, suppression representation.
Event-driven cadence needs a trigger owner (systemd timer, deploy hook, Heimdall collector) — none named.
Cross-repo scanning needs explicit provenance: repo, commit/ref, check class, timestamp, scanner version.
Narrowing scope undermines the need for a separate component identity. May just be 'Grimnir infrastructure maintenance.'
Scan script should live in grimnir repo under scripts/, not in Munin or a new repo.
'Build the simplest thing' is rhetorical without naming the exact smallest slice (e.g., npm audit + secret scan only).
